steps.title: Identify the example-illustration pattern
steps.content: The first sentence describes demonstration sports generally — they didn't award medals. The second sentence provides a specific instance: glima at the 1912 Stockholm Olympics. The blank introduces that specific example.

steps.title: Match the example transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is example. The blank needs a transition signaling that the second sentence illustrates the general claim about demonstration sports.
  • Choice A — For example,: signals a specific instance that illustrates a general claim, so fits — glima is one such demonstration sport. ✓
  • Choice B — Meanwhile,: signals two things happening at the same time, but glima at 1912 isn't a simultaneous parallel event — it's an illustration. ✗
  • Choice C — Someday,: signals a future possibility, but glima's 1912 appearance is a historical fact, not a future hope. ✗
  • Choice D — On the other hand,: signals contrast, but glima doesn't contradict the first sentence — it exemplifies it. ✗
That matches Choice A — For example,.
